name: Make Meaningful Distinctions
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 2: Meaningful Names
problem: Identifiers differ in name only by noise words, numeric suffixes, or minor misspellings, providing no information about how they differ in purpose, making the codebase confusing and hard to navigate.
steps: Remove noise words such as Info, Data, Object, or The from names when they add no meaning.
Replace number-series names (a1, a2) with descriptive names that reveal intent (source, destination).
Ensure that if two names differ, they mean something different; never rename just to satisfy a compiler.
Verify each name can be distinguished from related names by reading them aloud.
Eliminate redundant qualifiers where the base name is already self-explanatory.
